title: Clippy: 4 function-call-inside-unwrap_or (eager evaluation bug risk)
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension:** Functional Correctness, Performance
**Lint:** clippy::or_fun_call

## Violation
`x.unwrap_or(some_function())` evaluates `some_function()` UNCONDITIONALLY, even when `x` is `Some` — the unwrap never uses the value. This is wasted work at best; at worst it's a bug (side effects, expensive allocation, or panics in the default path fire even on the happy path).

## Locations
- src/pipeline.rs: 138, 139, 299
- src/vdd/mod.rs: 1040

Additionally: `or(fn())` same category:
- src/tui/app.rs: 143

## Mandated Refactor
Replace eager `unwrap_or(f())` with lazy `unwrap_or_else(|| f())`:
```rust
// Before (eager, always runs f())
x.unwrap_or(expensive_default())
// After (lazy, only runs f() if x is None)
x.unwrap_or_else(|| expensive_default())
```
Same fix for `.or(f())` → `.or_else(|| f())`. Each of the 5 sites requires a case-by-case audit — if `f()` is a pure `const` literal or trivial value, `unwrap_or` is fine; only when it's a fn call/allocation/computation is this wasteful.
